title: Objects should not be created to be dropped immediately without being used
description: Objects should not be created to be dropped immediately without being usedAnalyze your codeintentionality-clearreliabilityBugWhy is this an issue?How can I fix it?Creating objects that are not used is a vulnerability that can lead to unexpected behavior.If this was done intentionally due to side effects in the object’s constructor, the code should be moved to a dedicated method.Available In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect issues in your GitHub, Azure DevOps Services, Bitbucket Cloud, GitLab repositoriesAnalyze code in youron-premise CIAvailable Since9.1Analyze code in youron-premise CIDeveloper EditionAvailable Since9.1©2025SonarSource Sàrl. All rights reserved.Privacy Policy|Cookie Policy|Terms of Use
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

logic.reasoning: Detects object instantiation statements that create objects but don't assign them to variables or use them immediately. Uses MethodDeclaration nodes to find standalone 'new Class();' statements. Excludes assignments, method chaining, return statements, constructor parameters, and test contexts where unused object creation might be intentional.
